content.full_text:
September 25, 1979

Ms. Julie Watt, Treasurer
California College Republicans
P.O. Box 75466
Los Angeles, California 90075

Dear Ms. Watt:

I am responding to your request for advice of September 7, 1979, concerning the filing obligations of the California College Republicans under the Political Reform Act.

I have been informed by Anthony Miller, Chief Counsel for the Secretary of State, that the Secretary of State's Office has determined to grant you a waiver of the fine imposed in their letter of February 27, 1979, to Britta Skavdahl. The Secretary of State's Office will notify you directly and officially of this action.

Assuming your fine to be waived there would appear to be no further matter on which you need advice from us at this time.

I would, however, suggest that you look into Government Code Sections 82015 and 82025, the definitions of "contribution" and "expenditure", which determine whether a person or group becomes a committee with filing obligations under Government Code Sections 82013 and 84100-84305.

The above definitions of "contribution" and "expenditure" are further defined by regulations in 2 Cal. Admin. Code Sections 18215 and 18225.

Briefly, if you do not receive or make payments for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or candidates or the qualification or passage of any measure and if you do not make payments used for communications which expressly advocate the nomination, election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or candidates or the qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified ballot measure - if, in other words, your activity is confined solely to the maintenance of your membership

Ms. Julie Watt                                    -2-                    September 25, 1979


organization and its generalized political objectives - then you
would very likely be making and receiving no contributions or
expenditures and would incur no reporting obligations under the
Act.

     If this is the case, you have erroneously filed a statement
of organization as a recipient committee. There is no need for
an organization that is not in fact a recipient committee to have
an identification number for any Political Reform Act purpose.
If you wish, you may terminate your status as a filed committee
by completing Form 415 and sending it to the Secretary of State.

     I hope this advice has been helpful. If you are still
uncertain about whether the particular facts of your organization's
activity relieve you of filing obligations, you may call me at
916/322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        John D. Giansello
                                        Political Reform Analyst

JDG:bw

California College Republicans

September 7, 1979

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 907
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Sirs:

I am writing to ask your opinion on the situation of
the California College Republicans and our status
with the Secretary of State's Office.

We applied for a Political I.D. number earlier this
year. According to the Secretary of State's office
we filed late and a fine of $100.00 was imposed on our
organization. Enclosed is a copy of our application
for the Political I.D. number, the letter from the
Secretary of State's office regarding the fine, and our
request for a waiver of the fine.

We only wanted an Identification number to give to
the contributors to our organization if they requested
one. The purpose of the California College Republicans
is to promote and encourage involvement in the Republican
Party on College campuses. We do not contribute to in-
dividual campaigns or candidates. Therefore, under
Proposition 9, are we required to file periodic reports
with the Secretary of State and if so, what must these
reports contain.

Our political identification number is 790245. Please
send an opinion on this matter to me at the following address:

California College Republicans
Julie Watt, Treasurer
P.O. Box 75466
Los Angeles, California 90075

Thank you,

Julie Watt

Julie Watt

1230 J Street

Sacramento, California 95807

POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION
(916) 322-4880

February 27, 1979

Ms. Britta M. Skavdahl, Treasurer
The California College Republicans, ID#790245
10813 Escobar
San Diego, CA 92124

Dear Ms. Skavdahl:

Thank you for filing your Recipient Committee Statement of Organization
(Form 410), executed on February 3, 1979. This letter is to assign your committee
identification number and to notify you of the liability for filing your statement
after the specified deadline.

ASSIGNMENT OF COMMITTEE IDENTIFICATION NUMBER

Your committee's identification number is 790245. All correspondence and
future campaign disclosure statements must include this identification number.
Your committee should also advise candidates and committees to which you
make contributions or expenditures of your identification number.

NOTICE OF LATE FILING LIABILITY

Form 410 requires that "date qualified as committee" be provided. Committees
must file a Statement of Organization with our office within 10 days of the
date on which they qualify.

Explanation of "Date Qualified"

"Date qualified" refers to the date on which your committee received
contributions totalling $500 or more during the calendar year. If your
committee did not qualify on the date you indicated, you should complete
the enclosed Form 405 (Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement)
and report the exact date (day, month, year) on which your committee
qualified.

If your committee has not yet qualified but needs a committee identification
number, you should complete the enclosed Form 405 and indicate that
the committee has not yet qualified. IT IS NOT NECESSARY TO FILE
ANOTHER STATEMENT OF ORGANIZATION WHEN THE COMMITTEE
DOES QUALIFY.

According to the date you indicated on your Statement of Organization, you
did not, as required by law, file your statement within ten days of qualification
and therefore you have been fined.

Ms. Britta M. Skavdahl
February 27, 1979
Page 2


Committees filing a statement after a specified deadline are liable in the amount
of $10 per day for each day late, up to a maximum fine of $100. YOU HAVE
INCURRED A LIABILITY OF $100.00. The Political Reform Act permits waiver
of the liability in certain limited cases. If your Statement of Organization
was filed late, you may complete the enclosed "Request for Waiver". We will
notify you of the results of your request.

If you have any questions pertaining to this letter, please contact this office.
If your questions are of an interpretive nature, please contact the Technical
Assistance and Analysis Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission
at (916) 322-5662 in Sacramento or (213) 620-5196 in Los Angeles.

Sincerely,

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform

GENE R. MORROW, Auditor
Political Reform Division

cc: Fair Political Practices Commission

REQUEST FOR WAIVER OF LIABILITY
(Government Code Section 91013)

(1) NAME OF LATE FILER California College Republicans

(2) ADDRESS OF LATE FILER 10217 Asobar San Diego, CA. 92124

(3) TELEPHONE NUMBER OF LATE FILER 714-560-0026

(4) TYPE OF STATEMENT FILED LATE Statement of Organization(Recipient)

(5) PERIOD COVERED ON STATEMENT

(6) DATE STATEMENT SHOULD HAVE BEEN FILED Not necessary

(7) HAVE YOU PREVIOUSLY FILED ANY STATEMENT OR REPORT UNDER THE
POLITICAL REFORM ACT AFTER A DEADLINE? YES ___ NO X_. IF YES,
IDENTIFY THE STATEMENT(S) FILED AFTER THE DEADLINE (e.g. 1976 post-
General Election, January 1977 lobbyist report).

(8) STATE IN FULL ON REVERSE SIDE THE REASON WHY THE STATEMENT WAS
FILED AFTER THE DEADLINE AND WHY YOU BELIEVE THE LIABILITY SHOULD
BE WAIVED.

I declare and certify under penalty of perjury that the foregoing information on this
request for waiver is true and correct. I hereby request that the liability for failing
to file a statement required by the Political Reform Act on time be waived.

EXECUTED ON ________March 21_________, 1979, AT ___Los Angeles_____,
                    (Month, day)                                                    (City)

California
(State)

                                                           __________________
                                                           (Signature)
                                                           Hunt Braly
                                                           (Type or print name)

FOR OFFICE USE ONLY

                                                           ACTION:

                                                           ___ Waived
NONFILER

                        _______________)

NONPAYMENT                                                   period (2nd pre-election only)
REFERRALS: ___No ___Yes (if yes, date referred
                   _______________)                      ___ Waived upon reconsideration

COMMENTS:                                                 ___ Denied upon reconsideration

                                                           ___ Reduced upon reconsideration

In a phone conversation with Mr. Gene Morrow, he indicated
to me that if we felt that there was no legal requirement for
the California College Republicans to file their that would or
a footnote ground for a waiver of the IRS fine imposed on
February 27, 1979.

The sole purpose for the California College Republican's
filing of an Organization statement was to obtain a political
ID number to give to contributors who requested it. We were
never informed, until my phone conversation with Mr. Morrow,
that a political ID number may be obtained even though you don't
meet the legal requirements of mandatory filing.

Through discussions with Mr. Allison for the Secretary of
State's office Los Angeles, the California College Republicans
has determined that it is not required to mandatorily file
a Statement of Organization for a Recipient Committee. Therefor
we request that the fine be waived.

identification number and to notify you of the liability for filing your statement

ASSIGNMENT OF COMMITTEE IDENTIFICATION NUMBER 
Your committee's identification number is 790245. All correspondence and

YOUr committee should also advise candidates and committees to which you 
make contributions or expenditures of your identification number. 
NOTICE OF LATE FILING LIABILITY 
Form 410 requires that "date qualified as committee" be provided. Committees

Explanation of "Date Qualified"

contributions totalling $500 or more during the calendar year. If your 
committee did not qualify on [he date you indicated, you should complete 
the enclosed Fonn 405 (Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement) 
and report the exact date (day, month, year) on which your committee 
qualified. 
--
If your committee has not yet qualified but needs a committee identification 
number, you should complete the enclosed Form 405 and indicate that 
the committee has not yet qualified. IT IS NOT NECESSARY TO FILE 
ANOTHER STATEMENT OF ORGANIZATION WHEN THE COMMITTEE 
DOES QUALIFY. 
According to the date you indicated on your Statement of Organization, you 
did not, as required by law, file your statement within ten days of qualification 
and therefore you have been fined. 


Britta 
Skavdahl 
February 27, L 979

sections.question_synthetic: The California College Republicans submitted a late Statement of Organization (Form 410) and are requesting waiver of the $100 fine incurred for filing after the 10-day deadline, arguing they were unaware a committee identification number could be obtained without mandatory filing.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document is primarily an incoming request for waiver and supporting forms rather than an FPPC advice letter. Contains late filing notification letter from Secretary of State and waiver request form. Quality of OCR varies; some text fragments are degraded. Document ID provided (79-017) matches 1979 date. No formal FPPC question and conclusion sections present.

Box 75466 Los Angeles, California 90075 Dear Ms. Watt: I am responding to your request for advice of September 7, 1979, concerning the filing obligations of the California College Republicans under the Political Reform Act. I have been informed by Anthony Miller, Chief Counsel for the Secretary of State, that the Secretary of State's Office has determined to grant you a waiver of the fine imposed in their letter of February 27, 1979, to Britta Skavdahl. The Secretary of State's Office will notify you directly and officially of this action. Assuming your fine to be waived there would appear to be no further matter on which you need advice from us at this time. I would, however, suggest that you look into Government Code Sections 82015 and 82025, the definitions of "contribution" and "expenditure", which determine whether a person or group becomes a committee with filing obligations under Government Code Sections 82013 and 84100-84305. The above definitions of "contribution" and "expenditure" are further defined by regulations in 2 Cal. Admin. Code Sections 18215 and 18225. Briefly, if you do not receive or make payments for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or candidates or the qualification or passage of any measure and if you do not make payments used for communications which expressly advocate the nomination, election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or candidates or the qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified ballot measure - if, in other words, your activity is confined solely to the maintenance of your membership Ms. Julie Watt -2- September 25, 1979 organization and its generalized political objectives - then you would very likely be making and receiving no contributions or expenditures and would incur no reporting obligations under the Act. If this is the case, you have erroneously filed a statement of organization as a recipient committee. There is no need for an organization that is not in fact a recipient committee to have an identification number for any Political Reform Act purpose. If you wish, you may terminate your status as a filed committee by completing Form 415 and sending it to the Secretary of State. I hope this advice has been helpful. If you are still uncertain about whether the particular facts of your organization's activity relieve you of filing obligations, you may call me at 916/322-5901. Sincerely, John D. embedding.summary: This document package contains a Statement of Organization (Form 410) filed by the California College Republicans on February 3, 1979, along with official correspondence from the Secretary of State's Elections and Political Reform Division dated February 27, 1979 notifying them of late filing liability ($100 fine), and a Request for Waiver of Liability submitted by Hunt Braly on March 21, 1979.
